texto: There are very few of us who remember the day, the moment, when our childhood ends. For most of us, the sun sets on our innocence gradually, sliding down over the western horizon like a toboggan run down over a long, steep slope. We are never really conscious of the moment we reach the bottom of the slope; we just know that one day we wake up and the toboggan ride is over.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Jennifer Wixson: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “There are very few of us who remember the day, the moment, when our childhood ends. For most of u…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que familia, natural-world, personal-growth pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de termino e personal-growth.
